sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, reports that she has been experiencing kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She states that the pain has been up and down, but she believes the current stone has passed, with the last episode resolving approximately three days ago.
She denies any current pain or blood in her urine.
She has a history of hypertension and diabetes, with an A1c of 6.7.
She is currently taking 2.5mg of Norvasc daily for hypertension and 500mg of metformin daily for diabetes.
She reports that her blood sugars have been "pretty good" but does not regularly check them.
She also mentions having issues with insomnia, stating that she sometimes stays awake worrying about things but has tried meditation apps to help.
She admits to having a hard time giving up fast food and processed snacks due to a busy lifestyle.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
She has had multiple episodes of kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
Review of systems: No additional symptoms in other organs were mentioned.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure was 150/90.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs were clear bilaterally, and the heart exam revealed a grade 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdomen exam showed no tenderness to palpation or the CVA on the right side.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, with the current episode believed to be resolved, and is being referred to urology for further evaluation.
She also has hypertension, which is being managed with Norvasc, and diabetes, which is being managed with metformin.
Problem list: Kidney stones; hypertension; diabetes; insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed Percocet as needed for pain management during the kidney stone episode.
I referred the patient to urology for further evaluation of her recurrent kidney stones.
I recommended a urinalysis and urine culture to ensure that everything is cleared up.
I also prescribed a referral to a nutritionist to help her with dietary changes to manage her hypertension and diabetes.
I advised her to start taking her blood sugars before every meal to monitor her diabetes.
I prescribed 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in three months to follow up on her progress.
